evidences.title: Gemini 3 is Out — Weekly AI Newsletter (November 24th 2025)
evidences.content: ## 📚 Guides From The Web [...] Google introduces Gemini 3. Google introduces Gemini 3, its most advanced AI model, enhancing multimodal understanding and agentic capabilities. Gemini 3 excels in reasoning, achieving top scores across major AI benchmarks and supporting complex tasks in everyday life. Grok 4.1 is out. Grok 4.1 is now available on grok.com, X, and mobile apps, enhancing creative interactions and emotional intelligence. Achieving a 64.78% win rate over previous models, it ranks #1 in the LMArena leaderboard. It excels in reducing hallucinations, improving real-world usability with refined personality and reasoning abilities through enhanced reinforcement learning infrastructure. [...] Introducing Meta Segment Anything Model 3 (SAM 3). Meta introduces Segment Anything Model 3 (SAM 3), which uses open-vocabulary text and visual prompts to detect, segment, and track objects in images and videos. SAM 3 supports text, exemplar, and click-based prompts, enables interactive refinement, and achieves state-of-the-art segmentation performance while preserving SAM 2 capabilities.

evidences.title: Google Gemini 3.0 puts the market on notice - Aragon Research
evidences.content: Aragon Research Google Gemini 3.0 puts the market on notice November 19, 2025 Share on Twitter Share on Facebook Share on LinkedIn # Google Gemini 3.0 puts the market on notice Gemini 3.0 By Jim Lundy ## Google Gemini 3.0 puts the market on notice The technological landscape is undergoing a swift, AI-driven evolution. The recent unveiling of Google’s Gemini 3.0 model, coupled with the new Google Antigravity agentic development platform, signals a significant strategic acceleration by the vendor. This move is not merely an incremental update; it represents a deep commitment to an agent-first future. This blog overviews the Gemini 3.0 news and offers our analysis on the implications for the technology market and the enterprise. [...] ### Why did Google announce the Gemini 3.0 and Antigravity combination? Google’s release of Gemini 3.0 comes with impressive claims of state-of-the-art reasoning, multimodal understanding, and agentic coding capabilities, significantly outperforming its predecessors on critical benchmarks. Key features include [...] Evaluate the new Gemini 3.0 capabilities, especially the extended context window and multimodal reasoning, to see how they integrate with...

evidences.title: Google Aims to Lead AI Race with Gemini 3 Foundation Model
evidences.content: November 18, 2025 Pic of Google Gemini app This article was updated on 11/19/2025. Google on Tuesday unleashed Gemini 3, the broadest and most powerful expansion of its flagship generative AI foundation model family. The wide-ranging and much-anticipated model release arrived on the same day that Google's rival tech giant, Microsoft, launched a dramatic expansion of its own generative AI arsenal in the form of an extensive agentic AI suite, and follows a series of product and multi-billion-dollar data center dealmaking moves in the AI race by another adversary, independent AI vendor OpenAI. [...] Google said that key components of the new Gemini 3 family are immediately available, with some features in preview and others generally available. They include new AI tools in Vertex AI and Gemini Enterprise for businesses, the Gemini consumer app, and Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers in AI Mode in Search, as well as developers using the Gemini API in AI Studio, Antigravity and the Gemini CLI command-line interface. By focusing on two major fronts -- advancing Search with more AI and agentic commerce capabilities and targeting the business market with more reasoning and multimodal f...

evidences.title: Google Gemini 3.0 is coming: What we know - Aragon Research
evidences.content: Google Gemini 3.0 is coming: What we know October 29, 2025 # Google Gemini 3.0 is coming: What we know By Jim Lundy ## Google Gemini 3.0 is coming: What we know Google CEO Sundar Pichai’s announcement at Dreamforce 2025—that Gemini 3.0 will be released before the end of this year—has set the industry on a highly compressed and competitive clock. This timeline comes just months after the major Gemini 2.5 release in early 2025 and poses an immediate question: Is this a genuine generational leap fueled by Google’s massive research infrastructure, or is it a strategic move to maintain market momentum against rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic? This blog overviews the Gemini 3.0 news and offers our analysis. ### Why did Google announce Gemini 3.0 with a year-end deadline? [...] While the official launch date remains elusive (many speculate late December), the model’s arrival is already being signaled through “quiet” releases. Some users on the Gemini Advanced subscription have reportedly seen notifications of an upgrade to the “3.0 Pro version,” with Google calling it the “smartest model yet.” This “deploy first, announce later” strategy mirrors Google’s past releases and allows them t...

evidences.title: Gemini 3.0 Has Arrived: Inside Google's Most Intelligent AI and the ...
evidences.content: google ## Part 1: The Arrival On the morning of November 18, 2025, while most of the world was busy scrolling through the usual chatter of trending hashtags and AI memes, Google quietly pressed the launch button on something that will likely go down in history books. No cinematic livestream. No live demo hall of clapping engineers. Just a blog post from Demis Hassabis and a subtle API version bump: > “Today, we’re introducing our most capable and intelligent AI model yet Gemini 3.0.” And just like that, the game changed again. [...] Sitemap Open in app Sign in Sign in ## Data And Beyond · Data And Beyond Selected stories around Data Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Programming, and Technology topics. evidences.title: [PDF] Gemini 3 Pro Frontier Safety Framework Report - Googleapis.com
evidences.content: Published: November 2025 Gemini 3 Pro Frontier Safety Framework Report Frontier Safety Framework Report – Gemini 3 Pro We released our Frontier Safety Framework (FSF) (v1) in May 2024 and updated it in February 2025 (v2) and September 2025 (v3). The FSF comprises a set of protocols that aims to address severe risks that may arise from the high-impact capabilities of frontier AI models. It currently covers four risk domains where, based on early research, we have determined severe risk may be most likely to arise from future models: CBRN (chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear information risks), cybersecurity, machine learning R&D, and harmful manipulation, and also includes an exploratory approach for misalignment risk. Our FSF is built around capability thresholds called

evidences.title: Gemini 3 Is Here—and Google Says It Will Make Search Smarter
evidences.content: Google says that Gemini 3 will be rolled out to Google AI Plus and Google AI Pro subscribers, who pay $19.99 and $249.899 per month, respectively, in the coming weeks. The company is also launching a new AI programming tool called Antigravity that is powered by Gemini 3. Bubble or not, Hassabis says that Gemini 3 will be a platform for building more capable AI in the future. “I still think we are five to 10 years away from what I would call proper full AGI,” he says. “And that may require one or two breakthroughs on top of the models that are just getting better and better.” Update: 11/21/2025, 11:00 AM EDT: WIRED has corrected how Google is making Gemini 3 available, and has clarified which search queries increased using AI Overviews. ## You Might Also Like [...] Google is also using AI to build popular new tools like NotebookLM, which can auto-generate podcasts from written materials, and AI Studio which can prototype applications with AI. It’s even exploring embedding the technology into areas like gaming and robotics, which Hassabis says could pay huge dividends in years to come, regardless of what happens in the wider market. Google is making Gemini 3 available today throug...

evidences.title: Google Gemini 3.0 is coming: What we know - Aragon Research
evidences.content: While the official launch date remains elusive (many speculate late December), the model’s arrival is already being signaled through “quiet” releases. Some users on the Gemini Advanced subscription have reportedly seen notifications of an upgrade to the “3.0 Pro version,” with Google calling it the “smartest model yet.” This “deploy first, announce later” strategy mirrors Google’s past releases and allows them to gather crucial real-world performance data before mass deployment. Our Aragon Research clients who have been testing early-access variants have expressed significant optimism. The feedback indicates clear progress in areas that matter most for enterprise integration and automation. Specifically, the model shows improvements in three critical areas: Programming and Structural [...] Google Gemini: The core advantage is deep integration into Google Workspace and the Vertex AI platform. Enterprises already utilizing the Google stack gain the shortest time-to-value. The aggressive release cycle, however, demands constant vigilance for model regression or unexpected post-deployment behavior—the cost of Google’s speed is the enterprise’s burden of continuous testing. OpenAI GP...

evidences.content: This interface gives you insight into how Gemini 3.0 differs from its predecessors in real-time, making it an excellent learning opportunity for understanding AI model evolution. ## Best Practices for Testing Gemini 3.0 Once you have access, make the most of your testing sessions: ### Effective Test Prompts Try these categories to fully explore Gemini 3.0's capabilities: 1. Complex reasoning problems ``` "Explain quantum computing using only simple analogies that a 12-year-old could understand" ``` 2. Code generation challenges ``` "Write a Python script that analyzes sentiment in customer reviews and generates visual insights" ``` 3. Creative tasks [...] ### How to Use A/B Test of Gemini 3.0 Pro: 1. Go to AI Studio - Navigate to `aistudio.google.com/prompts/new_chat` 2. Spam your prompt - Send the same prompt 10-20 times continuously until you get the A/B test UI 3. Check Model ID - Even after getting the A/B interface, you might still get Flash or Pro models. The only way to confirm you're using Gemini 3.0 is by checking the Model ID (if you know, you know!) This method requires persistence but is currently the most effective way to access Gemini 3.0 features before public rel...

evidences.title: Gemini 3 and Antigravity, explained: Why Google's latest AI releases ...
evidences.content: Fortune 500 AIGoogle # Gemini 3 and Antigravity, explained: Why Google’s latest AI releases are a big deal By Dave Smith Dave Smith Former Editor, U.S. News By Dave Smith Dave Smith Former Editor, U.S. News November 19, 2025, 10:38 AM ET Google CEO Sundar Pichai gestures to the crowd during Google's annual I/O developers conference in Mountain View, California on May 20, 2025.Camille Cohen / AFP—Getty Images Google released Gemini 3 on Tuesday, rolling out what it calls its most advanced AI model across its entire ecosystem. The release also includes a new coding platform called Antigravity, and for the first time, Google integrated its newest Gemini model into Search on launch day.​ [...] Google is also releasing Gemini 3 Deep Think, an enhanced reasoning mode that further improves performance on complex problems. The mode achieved 41.0% on Humanity’s Last Exam without tools and 93.8% on GPQA Diamond—as you might imagine, both were best-in-class. Deep Think will become available to Google AI Ultra subscribers after additional safety testing.​

evidences.title: Google announces Gemini 3 as battle with OpenAI intensifies - CNBC
evidences.content: The new AI model will allow users to get better answers to more complex questions, "so you get what you need with less prompting," Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai said in one of several blog posts Google published Tuesday. Gemini 3 will be integrated into the Gemini app, Google's AI search products AI Mode and AI Overviews, as well as its enterprise products. The rollout begins Tuesday for select subscribers and will go out more broadly in the coming weeks. The announcement comes about eight months after Google introduced Gemini 2.5 and 11 months after Gemini 2.0. OpenAI, which kicked off the generative AI boom in late 2022 with the public launch of ChatGPT, introduced GPT-5 in August. [...] Google said the new model will enable "generative interfaces," providing certain answers in a way that resembles a digital magazine. As an example, the company asked Gemini to "explain the Van Gogh Gallery with life context for each piece." The result was a colorful, image-based explanation for each painting. Within AI Mode, Gemini 3 will first be available to paid subscribers. It will be able to analyze a question and create a layout with visual elements such as images, tables and grids. Google...

evidences.title: Getting Started with Gemini 3 Pro Image | Google Cloud Blog
evidences.content: Ready to claim your credits? Follow these steps: ### Step 1 - Get started Navigate to cloud.google.com/free. You should see a button that says "Start Free" or "Get Started for Free". Note: If you don’t see the ‘Get starter for Free’ button you most likely have activated the Cloud account in the past, but should still be able to leverage the Free Tier. ### Step 2 - Sign in Sign in using your Google Account (gmail). This can be the same account you used for Google AI Studio. Note: If you see a message saying “You may already have a Google Account yourname@gmail.com. Cancel to create a new account using an email address from a different provider.”, please click ‘Sign in’ to continue. ### Step 3 - Accept Terms of Service [...] The good news? It’s free to start, and provides you a stash of credits to play with. In this post, we’ll walk through exactly how to sign up for the Google Cloud Free Trial and, more importantly, what you actually receive. ## Why do I need a Cloud Account? Google AI Studio is fantastic for prototyping and experimenting, while also allowing you to deploy applications directly to Cloud Run. Nevertheless as your application grows, you may want to leverage the bro...

evidences.title: Gemini 3 Release Date Expectations 2025 Late Q4 Launch
evidences.content: ## When Will Gemini 3 Release? The most consistent signals point toward a late-2025 release window, with industry observers narrowing the likely launch period to the final months of the year. ### Sundar Pichai’s Confirmation Public comments from Sundar Pichai strongly suggest that Gemini 3 will launch before the end of 2025. In interviews discussing Google’s expanding AI infrastructure, he indicated that the company’s next-generation model is tied closely to major data-center deployments currently being completed. [...] ### Likely Launch Window: October to December 2025 Based on multiple industry reports and developer-community discussions, the most realistic Gemini 3 release window appears to be between October and December 2025. Several factors support this timeline: Insider discussions within developer communities have mentioned a narrower target window between mid-November and early December. This aligns with Google’s historical launch cadence and gives the company enough time to finalize infrastructure scaling. While an earlier release is technically possible, most analysts agree that late Q4 remains the most likely launch period. ## What Gemini 3 Is Expected to Bring ### A...
